rules.name: block-rm
rules.reason: Use recoverable deletion (`trash` or `gio trash`) instead of `rm`.

rules.name: block-find-delete
rules.reason: Destructive deletion is blocked. Use recoverable deletion workflows.

rules.name: block-git-push
rules.reason: Push overwrites remote. Commit locally and request review first.

rules.name: block-git-checkout
rules.reason: Checkout can lose uncommitted work or switch branches unexpectedly. Use 'git worktree add' for isolated changes.

rules.name: block-git-switch
rules.reason: Switching branches can lose uncommitted work. Use 'git worktree add' instead.

rules.name: block-git-branch
rules.reason: Branch modifications can cause confusion. Create feature branches via PR workflow instead.

rules.name: block-git-add-all
rules.reason: Stage specific files explicitly; avoid sweeping adds.

rules.name: block-git-stash
rules.reason: Stashing can lose track of changes. Commit work or use 'git worktree add' instead.

rules.name: block-git-stash-pop
rules.reason: Applying stashed work can cause conflicts. Commit your changes instead.

rules.name: block-git-revert
rules.reason: Revert loses commit history. Discuss with team before reverting commits.

rules.name: block-git-reset
rules.reason: Reset can lose commits permanently. Use 'git revert' instead if needed.

rules.name: block-git-clean
rules.reason: Clean permanently removes untracked files. Review changes manually instead.

rules.name: block-git-rebase
rules.reason: Rebase rewrites commit history. Use merge instead to preserve history.

rules.name: block-git-merge
rules.reason: Merge can introduce unwanted changes. Use PR workflow with code review instead.
